bullets: NSRP shipped 6,000-7,000t homo to China at 1,180-1,200 CIF as domestic oversupply persists.
PP 114G to 33,000 (32,800 for containers); BSR T3034 to 34,000 VND/kg.
SEA yarn -40 to 1,200, block copo -80 to 1,210; Vietnam prices the cheapest in the region.
LDPE finalized 1,400-1,450 CIF (down 50-70); Chinese BASF HDPE to 1,160.
Petrochina LLDPE 1,120-1,130; offers above 1,200 CIF struggle to find buyers.
LSP sideways a 4th week; SEA HDPE/LLDPE/LDPE all -20 USD/ton.
AGC's July offer landed early: K66 at 750-760 CIF, down 90-100 vs last month.
Sinopec S1000 finalized 700; Shintech SE950 690 CIF, the market low.
AGC Vina's PM66R July quote cut approx 2,000 VND/kg; China PVC -90 to 760.

editorial.changedNote: 10 of 50 moved down, led by PVC & PP

summary: Asian PE fell again, SEA the sharpest: HDPE -20, LLDPE/C6 -20, LDPE -20 USD/ton; NEA HDPE -10, LL/LD -15.
Finalized deals undercut offers: LDPE 1,400-1,450, Chinese BASF HDPE 1,160, Petrochina LLDPE 1,120-1,130; above 1,200 CIF struggles.
LSP held offers sideways a 4th week; domestic LLDPE 33,000-36,000 and HDPE 36,000-39,000 VND/kg, Chinese grades cheapest.
paras: Today, June 12, the finalized selling price of Lotte Hyundai's LDPE Film for Vietnamese customers has been announced at 1400 USD/ton/CIF; some customers buying smaller quantities were quoted a finalized price of 1420 USD/ton/CIF, lower by 50-70 USD/ton compared to the mid-week offer price. Similarly, the finalized import price of Lotte Titan Malaysia's LDPE Film 260GG for Vietnamese buyers has also just been announced at 1450 USD/ton/CIF, down 50 USD/ton compared to the mid-week offer price... Overall, the LDPE import level is at 1400-1450 USD/ton/CIF.

With HDPE Film, the finalized selling price of Chinese BASF's HDPE Film for Vietnamese buyers today was recorded at 1160 USD/ton/CIF, lower by more than 50 USD/ton compared to early-week offer prices. Lotte Hyundai's HDPE Film FL7000 and HDPE yarn MF5000 in the middle of this week were at 1270 USD/ton/CIF; some shared information that Lotte Hyundai's HDPE Film finalized transaction was recorded at 1180 USD/ton/CIF and SK South Korea's HDPE yarn finalized at 1200 USD/ton/CIF...

With LLDPE, a distributor offered import prices for LLDPE Film 222WT from the joint venture plant in China, Sabic Tianjin, at 1230 USD/ton/CIF Hai Phong, including CIC charges, but no finalized price has been recorded yet. In fact, this week, Chinese Petrochina's LLDPE Film 7042 price to Vietnam has decreased to 1120-1130 USD/ton/CIF; even some LLDPE Film grades from Saudi Arabia's Sabic were liquidated at 1150 USD/ton/CIF late last week... Therefore, offers above the 1200 USD/ton/CIF mark struggle to attract buyer attention.

Domestically, LSP has sold out of HDPE Film inventory after nearly 3 weeks of plant closure; currently LSP only has a small amount of HDPE blow for sale and a fair amount of LLDPE Film. This week, LSP continues to keep the offer prices of aforementioned PE grades unchanged (moving sideways for 4 consecutive weeks); but in actual trading, LSP distributors are selling with more flexible prices.

On the commercial market, by the end of this week, the common offer level for LDPE Film is in the range of 45,000-49,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, with the low-price segment mainly being American and Chinese exports.
The offer price level for various HDPE types commonly fluctuates from 36,000-39,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; in which American and Chinese-origin HDPE Film/blow items sit in the low-price segment.

The common offer price level for LLDPE Film/injection is from 33,000-36,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; in which high-quality LLDPE Film items from Sabic, Qamar, Exxon Mobil are traded at 35,500-36,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; Chinese-origin LLDPE Film has the cheapest price...

Broadening to the region, according to Platts pricing, in Northeast Asia, the price level of HDPE Film/ yarn/ injection/ blow this week simultaneously decreased by 10 USD/ton compared to last week, LLDPE and LDPE decreased by 15 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, the price level of HDPE Film/ yarn/ injection/ blow this week decreased by 20 USD/ton, fluctuating at 1290-1300 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE price decreased by 20 USD/ton to 1250 USD/ton/CFR, LLDPE metallocene C6 decreased by 20 USD/ton to 1400 USD/ton/CFR; LDPE price also decreased by 20 USD/ton to 1500 USD/ton/CFR.

According to pricing from another international organization, prices for various LDPE, LLDPE, HDPE grades in Asian regions this week also mostly decreased within the range of 10-40 USD/ton, depending on the segment.

Thus, Asian PE prices this week continued to decline, with the Southeast Asian region decreasing the most sharply. The reason is due to prolonged weak demand, reducing buyer interest in imported goods. Meanwhile, the supply is improving, notably with South Korean supply offering more back to Southeast Asia. Short-term prospects for PE remain quite gloomy, likely to weaken further due to intense competitive pressure.

Mid-week, although US-Iran tensions escalated after Iran declared it would completely close the Strait of Hormuz, this development has not yet had much impact on spot PE market trends. Another notable piece of information is that Malaysia's PRefChem is planning to restart PE plants in the second half of June 2026 after more than 3 months of suspension due to upstream unit issues.

summary: SEA PP slid hardest: yarn/inj -40 to 1,200, BOPP -50 to 1,220, block copo -80 to 1,210; Vietnam -30-40 and lowest in region.
NSRP shipped 6,000-7,000t homo to China at 1,180-1,200; PP 114G to 33,000 (32,800 for containers), BSR T3034 to 34,000 VND/kg.
Korean off-grade copo down to 30,500-31,500; South copo offers 36,000-38,000; Lotte M9600 coating to 46,000 VND/kg.
paras: According to the summary from sources today, June 12, large distributors of Nghi Son Refinery and Petrochemical (NSRP) Vietnam have exported approximately 6,000-7,000 tons of PP homo to the Chinese market this week, with finalized prices ranging from 1180-1200 USD/ton/CIF.

Long Son Petrochemicals (LSP), after almost 3 weeks of closing the plant, currently only has a small amount of PP copo inventory (out of PP homo inventory). The offer price this week continues to move sideways (no adjustment for 4 consecutive weeks), but finalized selling prices from distributors are more flexible.

Overall, although LSP paused production, the domestic PP homo market in Vietnam is in oversupply because production output from other domestic plants remains strong. Offer sources from China have recently lacked price competitiveness, but PP import shipments from South Korea are gradually returning, keeping competition intense. In the context where domestic PP inventory in Vietnam is still high and domestic prices are the cheapest in the region, large suppliers recently have been actively pushing exports to the region.

Regarding price movements, today, June 12, sources in the North said that some PP 114G transactions from NSRP were recorded at 33,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, down 400-500 VND/kg compared to the beginning of the week. Even large container buyers could finalize at 32,800 VND/kg/ with VAT. This is almost the cheapest PP yarn price item in the market at this time.

BSR's PP T3034 goods also dropped very sharply. Today, a source in the South said the Ho Chi Minh City area recorded PP T3034 transaction prices down to 34,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, although early this week the common offer price was around 35,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. Also in the South, Hyosung Vina has not issued new offer prices today.
Imported PP items from China or Russia's Sibur mostly kept prices sideways compared to the beginning of the week.

Regarding PP copo and random, in the North, a distributor in the middle of this week offered South Korea Lotte's PP random copo J560s at 37,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, a sharp drop of up to 8,000 VND/kg compared to their own offer price in the middle of May. The offer price dropped deeply in the context of intense competition, as well as South Korea's off-grade (OG) PP copo goods having transaction prices down to only 30,500-31,500 VND/kg/ with VAT.

In the South, PP copo offer prices (including all origins) are commonly fluctuating in the range of 36,000-38,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, down sharply compared to last week. South Korea Lotte's PP coating M9600 goods last week had the lowest offer at 48,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, but this week continued to drop, with the lowest recorded finalized price at 46,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. Hyosung Vina's PP random originating from Vietnam is offered in the range of 46,000-47,000 VND/kg/ with VAT...

Broadening to the region, according to Platts pricing announced today, June 11, in Northeast Asia, the price level of PP yarn/ injection/ block copo this week remained stable while BOPP and IPP decreased by 10 USD/ton compared to last week. In Southeast Asia, the price level of PP yarn/ injection this week dropped sharply by 40 USD/ton to 1200 USD/ton/CFR, BOPP dropped by 50 USD/ton to 1220 USD/ton/CFR, IPP Film dropped by 20 USD/ton to 1270 USD/ton/CFR, especially PP block copo dropped sharply by 80 USD/ton down to 1210 USD/ton/CFR (in Vietnam, PP prices this week simultaneously dropped 30-40 USD/ton and are the lowest in the region).

According to pricing from another international organization, the price level of PP types in Northeast Asia and India this week all remained stable compared to last week; but in Southeast Asia and remaining South Asian countries, a decrease of 10-20 USD/ton was recorded.

Thus, the Asian PP market this week moved sideways to down, with the Southeast Asian region witnessing the sharpest decline. Fundamental factors of weak demand and raw material costs continue to put pressure on the market. Because PP output in China is lower (plants are still in maintenance season), domestic PP prices in China mostly moved sideways. There is even news that Chinese traders have begun importing PP from Vietnam because commodity prices are more competitive compared to domestic offer prices in that country.

summary: AGC offered July early: K66 to Vietnam 750-760 CIF (-90-100 m/m), K58 765, 740 for 1,000t+ orders.
Finalized imports slid: Sinopec S1000 700, LG/Hygain 700 EXW, Shintech SE950 690 CIF (market low).
China PVC -90 to 760 CFR; AGC Vina PM66R July quote cut approx 2,000 VND/kg; oil-based Chinese PVC down to 22,500.
paras: Following Formosa Taiwan, today, June 12, AGC Group, a leading PVC manufacturer in Asia, also offered PVC for July 2026 shipments to the Vietnamese market, earlier than the monthly custom (usually AGC offers at the end of the 3rd week of the month).

Accordingly, the offer price for PVC K66 from AGC’s two plants, Vinythai Thailand (AVT) and Ashahimas Indonesia (ASC), to Vietnam this month is at 750-760 USD/ton/CIF depending on the volume, down 90-100 USD/ton compared to last month. The offer price for PVC K58 is at 765 USD/ton/CIF. The distributor also offers incentives for customers buying large quantities over 1000 tons with a price of 740 USD/ton/CIF.

Also according to the update today, the finalized import price for PVC S1000 from China’s Sinopec was recorded at 700 USD/ton/CIF Hai Phong, lower by 20 USD/ton compared to the mid-week offer price. The finalized prices for PVC TL-1000 originating from LG Chem (plant located in China) and HS-1000R of Hygain brand (manufactured by China’s Haijing) at Hai Phong bonded warehouse are both at 700 USD/ton/EXW, down 20 USD/ton compared to last week.

Regarding American goods, at Hai Phong port, the finalized selling price for PVC SE950/ SE1000 from America’s Shintech for Vietnamese buyers this week was recorded at 690 USD/ton/CIF, the lowest in the market currently.

In domestic trading, today, there is news that Vietnam's AGC Vina manufacturer also issued early quotes to some large customers for July 2026 shipments, with the offer price for grade PVC PM66R dropping sharply by about 2,000 VND/kg compared to last month (quoted on May 22). We will continue to update further early next week. In domestic trading, the offer price for PVC SG-5 (coal-based) originating from China is commonly in the range of 21,500-22,500 VND/kg/ with VAT.

For oil-based PVC goods, the price decline is even more powerful. Some sellers have lowered the offer price for Chinese-origin oil-based PVC to 22,500 VND/kg/ with VAT, the narrowest price gap with coal-based PVC since early March 2026. Consequently, many oil-based PVC items of other origins also continue to face downward price pressure, with most trading around 33,000-34,500 VND/kg/ with VAT...

Broadening to the region, according to Platts pricing, the PVC price level in China this week dropped sharply by 90 USD/ton compared to last week, down to 760 USD/ton/CFR; the PVC price level in Southeast Asia decreased by 40 USD/ton down to 760 USD/ton/CFR.

According to pricing from another international organization, the PVC price level in China, Southeast Asia, and India this week decreased by 20-30 USD/ton in the low-end segment and decreased sharply by 60 USD/ton in the high-end segment (the price level in Southeast Asia fluctuated at 770-790 USD/ton/CFR).

Asian PVC prices decreased during the week due to weak demand and lower July offer prices from major suppliers, especially Formosa Taiwan’s July PVC price to the region dropping 115-120 USD/ton compared to last month. Some customers are waiting for new quotes from other manufacturers, and market sentiment remains quite cautious. Furthermore, shipping lines from China increased freight rates in June to a very high level, slowing down PVC export activities.
